description_long: This is a medium-sized, bulbous vase with a rounded body that tapers toward a narrow neck and flares out at the rim. The surface is matte and earthy, with a brown coloration and subtle greenish patches that suggest weathering or moss-like patina. The rim is thick and slightly overhangs the neck, providing a defined lip for the opening. The vase appears to be made of ceramic or clay, with a solid, hand-crafted look and no visible handles or decorative reliefs. Its form is simple and functional, suitable for holding flowers or as a standalone decorative piece. The overall appearance suggests it would blend well in outdoor or rustic urban settings, such as parks or garden courtyards.
description: A matte, brown, bulbous ceramic vase with a flared rim and subtle greenish weathering, giving it a rustic, natural look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full rounded body and the thick, flared rim, with subtle greenish patches visible on the surface.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the smooth curvature of the vase and the slightly uneven coloration, with more shadow on the right.
description_view_2: The back view reveals a slightly darker area near the neck and more pronounced greenish weathering on the upper body.
description_view_3: This side view is similar to view 1 but with less visible greenish patina and a more uniform brown surface.
